name: Umm al-Banin
title: Mother of the Water Bearer
shortDescription: The devoted wife of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) whose four sons were martyred in Karbala, and who created a storm in Medina with her elegies.
fullStory:
Fatima bint Hizam, known as Umm al-Banin, was the loyal wife of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and the mother of four heroes of Karbala: Hazrat Abbas, Abdullah, Uthman, and Jafar. She was a deeply pious, brave woman from a noble lineage.

When Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan left Medina, Umm al-Banin stayed behind due to old age, but sent her four sons with the Imam, advising them never to leave Imam Hussain (AS) alone.

After the event of Ashura, when Bashir brought the news of the Karbala martyrs to Medina, Umm al-Banin asked before inquiring about her own sons: 'What news do you have of Hussain? May my sons be sacrificed for Hussain!' When Bashir broke the news of her four sons' martyrdom, she replied with unparalleled patience: 'You have torn the veins of my heart; may all my sons be sacrificed for Aba Abdillah.'

Every day, Umm al-Banin would go to the Baqi cemetery, draw four graves for her sons in the dirt, and recite such heart-rending elegies that even Marwan ibn Hakam (a fierce enemy of the Ahl al-Bayt) would weep upon hearing her voice.

Through these tears and elegies, she kept the message of Ashura alive in Medina and exposed the corrupt government.
quoteArabic: لا تَدْعُوِنِّي وَيْكِ أُمَّ البَنينَ، تُذَكِّرينِي بِلُيُوثِ العَرِينِ...
quote: (From her elegies:) Woe unto you! Do not call me Umm al-Banin anymore, for you remind me of the lions of my thicket...
